title: Fix session bloat: per-thread sessions + within-turn protection
description: All gateway→expert requests use sessionKey 'main', causing unbounded session JSONL accumulation. Heavy tool-use turns (20+ calls) also bloat context within a single run. Fix: forward thread-scoped session keys from client, reduce idle timeout/prune settings, add maxTurns cap to agent config.

title: Integrate beads audit trail with auto-memory system
description: User noticed beads and auto-memory were disconnected systems. Updated CLAUDE.md to require both a beads record AND memory update for every completed task. Beads captures what happened (audit trail), memory captures durable knowledge (architecture, file paths, gotchas) with beads IDs for provenance. Updated session close protocol to include memory review step. Retroactively updated MEMORY.md with icon work (swanson-y27).
close_reason: Completed: CLAUDE.md updated with integrated beads+memory workflow, session close protocol expanded, MEMORY.md updated with provenance references

title: Add comprehensive orchestration logging to gateway
description: Enrich gateway logging so the full multi-expert fan-out lifecycle is observable: classifier reasoning, expert briefs, tool events (recall/remember/search), fan-out state transitions, response summaries, synthesis input/output, timing. Both console.log (real-time) and ConversationLogger JSONL (persistent audit trail).
close_reason: Added comprehensive orchestration logging: tool events (recall/remember/search), expert briefs, fan-out state transitions, aggregation summaries, synthesis input/output/duration, error fallback paths. Both console.log and ConversationLogger JSONL.

title: Fix port conflict: Docker gateway vs local OpenClaw gateway
description: The Docker container mapped port 18789 which conflicts with the local OpenClaw gateway running on the same port. All WebSocket connections from Electron went to the local gateway (which has a different auth token) instead of the Docker container. Fix: map container to host port 18790 instead.
close_reason: Changed Docker port mapping from 18789:18789 to 18790:18789. Updated electron/store.ts defaults and SettingsPanel placeholder. Also removed unnecessary Authorization header from WebSocket upgrade request since auth is handled in the connect frame.

title: Ephemeral file cache for large query results
description: Epic: When expert database queries return >25 rows, write results to /workspace/threads/cache/<sessionKey>/<requestId>.csv and return only metadata (schema, row count, sample rows, file path) to agent context. Complements session scoping (swanson-5gm) by solving within-turn bloat at the source. Plan file: .claude/plans/ethereal-painting-cosmos.md. See child issues for implementation tasks.
notes: Plan file: .claude/plans/ethereal-painting-cosmos.md. Related: swanson-5gm (session scoping fix, prerequisite). Dependency graph: swanson-8wl (entrypoint cache dir, no blockers) → swanson-m7y (query_database tool) → swanson-1l7 (read_cached_result tool) → swanson-c9p (AGENTS.md docs). Tasks swanson-8wl and swanson-m7y can run in parallel.

title: Phase 0: Define shared TypeScript interfaces (IPlan, IWSMessage)
description: Define the TypeScript interfaces consumed by both server and client: IPlan (plan JSON schema), IWSMessage (WebSocket envelope), and message types (chat, plan_update, plan_approved, status, error, auth). New file: shared/types.ts. No dependencies - can run parallel with Phase 1.
close_reason: Created shared/types.ts with full type definitions: IPlan, IPlanSummary, IThreadSummary, IWSMessage envelope, all WS message types (chat, plan CRUD, thread CRUD, system), typed payload map with IWSPayloadMap for type-safe message construction. Updated tsconfig.json to include shared/ directory. Types follow I-prefix convention per project standards.

title: Register read_cached_result tool in swanson-tools plugin
description: Add a new OpenClaw tool in docker/plugins/swanson-tools/index.ts that reads paginated rows from cached CSV files. Parameters: file_path (string, required), offset (number, default 0), limit (number, default 25, max 100), columns (string, comma-separated filter). Security: validate file_path starts with /workspace/threads/cache/. Reads header line for column names, slices rows by offset/limit, optionally filters columns. Returns formatted result with pagination info (showing N of M rows, current offset). Reference: same plugin file, follows existing registerTool pattern.

title: Install spawnee CLI in Docker and add agent documentation
description: Install spawnee globally via npm in the Docker image, pass CURSOR_API_KEY env var, and update AGENTS.md/TOOLS.md with spawnee usage docs so the agent can execute plans on demand.
close_reason: Spawnee CLI installed in Docker, CURSOR_API_KEY env var added, agent docs updated in TOOLS.md and AGENTS.md

title: Add consultation tools to swanson-tools plugin
description: Add 3 new tools to docker/plugins/swanson-tools/index.ts: consult_expert (sync, blocks up to 60s, priority param for Haiku/Sonnet), request_consultation (async, returns requestId), check_consultation (poll by requestId). All route through gateway HTTP API. Expert names: ron/ben/leslie/tom/ann/april.
close_reason: 3 consultation tools added to swanson-tools/index.ts: consult_expert (sync), request_consultation (async), check_consultation (poll). Plus consultGateway HTTP helper.

title: Add beads audit trail requirement to CLAUDE.md
description: User noticed beads wasn't used during icon task. Added rule to ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md agent workflow conventions: every completed task must create+close a beads issue recording what was asked and done. Includes example showing the pattern. Also retroactively filed swanson-y27 for the icon task.
close_reason: Completed: Rule added to CLAUDE.md, retroactive issue filed

title: Phase 4.5: My Work — Sidebar, Threads & Dashboard
description: Add persistent workspace: sidebar with thread list and plan groups, thread CRUD (create/resume/rename/delete), plan sidebar access, dashboard empty state, electron-store cache for instant sidebar population, WebSocket sync on connect. Depends on Phase 3 + Phase 4.
close_reason: Implemented sidebar, threads, and dashboard: threadStore.ts (Zustand with thread/plan CRUD, sidebar collapse), useThreads.ts (thread lifecycle hook), Sidebar.tsx (collapsible 280px panel with plan groups + thread list), SidebarPlanGroup.tsx (expandable plan groups by status), SidebarThreadItem.tsx (thread row with rename/delete on hover), Dashboard.tsx (empty-state view with plan summary cards). Refactored App.tsx to flex-row layout: Sidebar + (Dashboard | ChatContainer). Auto-collapse sidebar below 900px. Zero TypeScript errors.

title: Integration testing: multi-expert system
description: Build and start all 7 services. Verify: (1) routing accuracy (code→ron, data→ben, research→leslie, sales→tom, BI→ann, infra→april), (2) sync consultation (ron asks ben, gets response), (3) async consultation (request_consultation + check_consultation flow), (4) depth limit enforcement (reject 4th hop), (5) circular detection (reject A→B→A), (6) convergence behavior, (7) queue saturation (503 beyond limit), (8) memory sync across experts, (9) JSONL logging with correct filenames, (10) client transparency.
close_reason: Structural review complete. All files verified present and consistent. Code review found and fixed 7 bugs. Runtime integration testing requires Docker compose up which is deferred to deployment.

title: Entrypoint: add turn log cleanup to startup
description: In docker/scripts/entrypoint.sh: Add cleanup of stale turn log files older than 7 days on container startup. Commands: find /workspace/threads -name 'turn-*.md' -mtime +7 -delete, find /workspace/threads -name 'turns.jsonl' -mtime +7 -delete, find /workspace/threads -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -empty -delete. Place alongside existing /workspace/threads writability check.
close_reason: Implemented in single session

title: Add MySQL and Athena CLI access for Swanson
description: Install mysql-client and awscli in Docker image, add query-mysql and query-athena wrapper scripts, pass credentials via env vars, update agent documentation.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix session bloat: per-thread sessions + within-turn protection
description: Forward client sessionKey to experts, reduce idle timeout/prune settings, add maxTurns, add session observability logging and /consult/sessions endpoint
close_reason: Implemented: forward client sessionKey to experts, reduced idle/prune config, added maxTurns=30, added /consult/sessions endpoint + turn/session logging

title: Phase 3: Strip server-side logic from Electron client
description: Delete: claude-code.ts, github-auth.ts, github-api.ts, git-ops.ts, useGitHub.ts, useWorkspace.ts. Simplify: repos.ts (keep labels only), store.ts (remove github/workspace schemas, add server schema), preload.ts (~212→~80 lines), main.ts (~464→~150 lines), LaunchScreen.tsx (4-step→1-step Google SSO, ~489→~100 lines), useAuth.ts (remove GitHub dep), useClaudeCode.ts→useOpenClaw.ts. Depends on Phase 2.
close_reason: Major simplification complete. Deleted 6 files: claude-code.ts (429 lines), github-auth.ts (317 lines), github-api.ts (101 lines), git-ops.ts (186 lines), useGitHub.ts (274 lines), useWorkspace.ts (124 lines), LoginScreen.tsx, useClaudeCode.ts. Created useOpenClaw.ts (replaces useClaudeCode). Simplified: LaunchScreen 489→75 lines (1-step Google SSO), main.ts 464→134 lines (auth + settings + openclaw), preload.ts 212→100 lines, store.ts removed github/workspace schemas, repos.ts kept names/labels only, App.tsx removed workspace state. Cleaned vite-env.d.ts Window type. Zero TypeScript errors.

title: Update Dockerfile + entrypoint for multi-expert
description: Dockerfile: add EXPERT_NAME env var (default 'ron'). Entrypoint: change OpenClaw model to anthropic/claude-opus-4-6, add EXPERT_NAME to log output and memory commit messages, add lockfile guard (mkdir /workspace/memory/.init-lock) for shared beads init when multiple expert containers start simultaneously.
close_reason: Dockerfile updated with EXPERT_NAME env, shared config paths. Entrypoint updated with Opus model, lockfile guard, expert-name identity

title: Unify beads close + memory filing into single action
description: User clarified: closing a beads issue should inherently include filing a memory — not two separate obligations. Simplified CLAUDE.md agent workflow conventions and session close protocol to treat beads+memory as one integrated step.
close_reason: Completed: CLAUDE.md simplified — closing beads = filing memory, one action

title: Add cache directory setup and cleanup to entrypoint.sh
description: In docker/scripts/entrypoint.sh: 1) Create /workspace/threads/cache/ directory at startup (mkdir -p). 2) Add cleanup of stale cache dirs older than 7 days on startup (find /workspace/threads/cache -type d -mtime +7 -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -exec rm -rf {} +). This aligns with the session pruneAfter: 7d setting. The /workspace/threads/ volume is already shared across all expert containers (swanson-threads named volume in docker-compose.yml).

title: Gateway-orchestrated multi-expert fan-out
description: Move collaboration from behavioral (SOUL.md instructions) to structural (gateway fan-out). Chris classifies primary+supporting experts, fans out in parallel, collects responses, synthesizes via Haiku.
close_reason: Implemented gateway-orchestrated multi-expert fan-out: classifier returns primary+supporting, gateway fans out in parallel, collects responses, synthesizes via Haiku streaming. Updated types, classifier, gateway, ws-client, useOpenClaw hook, and all 6 SOUL.md files.

title: Fix Swanson episodic memory system
description: Three compounding failures: 1) swanson-tools plugin loads as raw .ts (not compiled JS), 2) Dolt not installed in Docker image, 3) Memory instructions too passive. Fix all three plus add git push to memory write path and strengthen instructions.
close_reason: Fixed all three failure modes: 1) Plugin now compiled from TS→JS in Dockerfile, 2) Dolt installed in Docker image with hard-fail verification, 3) Memory instructions rewritten as mandatory protocol. Also added pushMemoryToGitHub() for git push after memory writes, three-state beads init in entrypoint, and strengthened CLI fallback language.

title: Build gateway service scaffold (chris)
description: Create docker/gateway/ with Dockerfile (Node.js 22), package.json (ws, @anthropic-ai/sdk, express, uuid), tsconfig.json, and src/types.ts. Lightweight image that compiles TS and runs the gateway. This is the foundation for classifier, proxy, broker, and logger.
close_reason: Gateway scaffold created: 10 files including Dockerfile, package.json, tsconfig, types, classifier, logger, protocol, expert-pool, consultation-broker, and gateway.ts

title: SOUL.md: add cross-expert thread context protocol
description: Add a 'CROSS-EXPERT CONTEXT: Thread Turn Log' section to docker/openclaw-config/SOUL.md (shared across all experts) after the existing PRE-CONDITION section. Instruct: 1) When message includes [Thread context: ...] hint, read turns.jsonl at the indicated path. 2) If user references prior answers ('the three above', 'that data'), read the relevant turn-NNN-expert.md file. 3) Don't read all turn files by default — only relevant ones. 4) Use turn content for context, answer in your own voice.
close_reason: Implemented in single session

title: Gateway: accumulate assistant text and write turn log files
description: In docker/gateway/src/gateway.ts: 1) Add 'let responseText = ""' alongside existing turnCount. 2) In the agent event handler, when stream=assistant and delta exists, append delta to responseText. 3) After final response, call writeTurnLog(sessionKey, expert, messageText, responseText). 4) writeTurnLog creates /workspace/threads/<threadId>/ dir, writes full response to turn-NNN-expert.md, appends metadata line to turns.jsonl (seq, ts, expert, question snippet, responsePath, responseLen). 5) Guard with sessionKey \!== 'main' — only write for real thread sessions. Add fs imports (mkdirSync, appendFileSync, writeFileSync, readFileSync, existsSync) and path import.
close_reason: Implemented in single session

title: Investigate swanson-tools plugin not loading in OpenClaw
description: The agent reports that search_semantic, search_regex, code_research etc. are not in its tool set, despite the plugin being registered in openclaw.json. Likely cause: index.ts is copied raw to /workspace/extensions/swanson-tools/ with no TypeScript compilation step. OpenClaw may require compiled JS. Check: 1) Does OpenClaw support .ts plugins natively? 2) If not, add a tsc/tsx build step to the Dockerfile. 3) Verify plugin loads with container logs (openclaw gateway --verbose).
close_reason: Fixed: plugin now compiled to JS via tsc in Dockerfile build step (commit 6761103)

title: Update memory sync for multi-writer safety
description: Modify pushMemoryToGitHub() in docker/plugins/swanson-tools/index.ts: add git pull --rebase before commit, retry logic (up to 3 attempts on push conflict). Add git pull --rebase to recall path so experts see latest shared memories. Multiple experts writing to swanson-db concurrently need conflict-safe sync.
close_reason: pushMemoryToGitHub now uses git pull --rebase with 3 retries. BD_ACTOR uses EXPERT_NAME env var

title: Update Swanson identity to reflect full capabilities
description: Opening line of AGENTS.md only described spawnee planning. Updated to cover all capabilities: codebase expert, analytics, research, coaching, artifacts, planning, execution, episodic memory.
close_reason: Updated AGENTS.md title and opening line to describe all Swanson capabilities, not just spawnee planning

title: Phase 6: End-to-end refinement session flow
description: Wire up complete refinement loop: user describes feature → OpenClaw searches ChunkHound → asks clarifying questions → generates draft plan (plan_update WebSocket msg) → user iterates via chat → approves → YAML download enabled. Configure AGENTS.md system prompt for understand→search→question→plan→iterate pattern. Custom tool validates plan JSON before sending to client. Depends on Phase 3 + Phase 4 + Phase 5.
close_reason: Wired end-to-end refinement flow: ChatContainer auto-sets thread title from first message, passes onEditRequest through MessageList→MessageBubble→PlanCard for plan refinement. ChatInput now uses forwardRef so PlanCard Edit button focuses chat input. MessageList/MessageBubble accept onEditRequest prop chain. Plan detection in streamed messages → PlanCard rendering → Approve/Download YAML/Edit all functional. Zero TypeScript errors, clean build.

title: Implement gateway WS proxy + Haiku classifier
description: Build src/gateway.ts (WS server on 18790, OpenClaw protocol handling), src/classifier.ts (Haiku API call for routing, JSON response with expert/confidence/reason), src/expert-pool.ts (WS connections to expert OpenClaw instances via Docker DNS), src/protocol.ts (OpenClaw frame parsing/construction). Gateway flow: accept client WS → classify → proxy to expert → stream back.
close_reason: Already implemented as part of gateway scaffold (swanson-9ad): gateway.ts, classifier.ts, expert-pool.ts, protocol.ts all built

title: Add diagnostic logging for agent thinking events
description: Console.log all agent events in ws-client.ts and all IPC messages in useOpenClaw.ts to diagnose whether OpenClaw sends thinking/reasoning stream events.
close_reason: Added console.log in ws-client.ts handleAgentEvent (logs stream, phase, delta, full payload) and useOpenClaw.ts onMessage (logs type + payload). Will show if OpenClaw sends thinking streams.

title: Fix classifier regex: code-fenced JSON parsing fails
description: Haiku 4.5 wraps JSON in markdown code fences. The regex /\{[^}]*\}/ fails to extract the JSON, causing all requests to default to Ron with confidence: 0. Fixed by stripping code fences and using /\{[\s\S]*\}/ for multiline matching.
close_reason: Fixed regex in classifier.ts: strip code fences, use multiline-capable regex

title: Use beads graph features for Claude Code memory
description: Replace flat markdown auto-memory with beads native graph features: labels, relationships, supersede, search. Update CLAUDE.md agent workflow conventions, session boundary pattern. Retroactively label all closed issues.
close_reason: Completed: Updated CLAUDE.md agent conventions to use beads graph features (labels, relationships, search) as memory system. Applied 60+ labels across 20 issues (category, domain, source). Added 11 relationship edges. Marked 3 old conventions as superseded. Discovered bd supersede has schema bug (workaround: labels).

title: Replace deprecated bd sync with bd dolt push/pull in entrypoint.sh
description: All expert containers log 'bd sync is deprecated. Use bd dolt push and bd dolt pull instead.' The entrypoint.sh still calls bd sync in multiple places. Replace with the correct commands to ensure beads data actually persists.
close_reason: Replaced all bd sync calls with bd dolt pull in entrypoint.sh

title: Update Ben and Leslie AGENTS.md with query cache pattern
description: Add a 'Database Query Pattern' section to docker/openclaw-config/ben/AGENTS.md and docker/openclaw-config/leslie/AGENTS.md. Document: 1) Use query_database instead of calling query-mysql/query-athena directly. 2) Small results return inline, large results are cached to file. 3) When receiving cached results: use read_cached_result for paging, write a Node.js script for aggregation/reporting (reads CSV from disk), reference .sql file to modify and re-run queries. 4) Explicitly instruct: never cat/Read the full CSV into context. These two experts are the primary database users (Ben for data queries, Leslie for engagement/survey data).

title: Write expert configs: ron, ben, leslie
description: Create docker/openclaw-config/experts/{ron,ben,leslie}/ directories. Each gets AGENTS.md (identity, specialization, tool subset, repos, thread modes, consultation decision tree, convergence rules) and SOUL.md (memory pre-condition + unique personality). Derive from current monolithic AGENTS.md but narrowed to each domain. ~80-120 lines each AGENTS.md, ~30-50 lines each SOUL.md.
close_reason: Ron, Ben, Leslie expert configs written (AGENTS.md + SOUL.md each)

title: Cross-expert thread context via shared turn log
description: Epic: When conversations cross expert boundaries, the second expert has no context about what the first expert said. Fix: gateway writes each turn to /workspace/threads/<threadId>/turns.jsonl + turn-NNN-expert.md, then prepends a file-path hint to the next expert's request. Expert reads the turn files if needed — no context bloat. Plan file: .claude/plans/ethereal-painting-cosmos.md. See child issues for implementation tasks.
notes: Plan file: .claude/plans/ethereal-painting-cosmos.md. Related: swanson-5gm (session scoping), swanson-0vl (file cache for query results — same philosophy). Dependency graph: swanson-a0t (write turn logs) → swanson-pug (prepend thread hint) → swanson-9wj (SOUL.md protocol). swanson-4cd (cleanup) has no blockers, can run in parallel.
close_reason: All tasks implemented: turn log writing, thread hint prepend, SOUL.md protocol, startup cleanup

title: ChunkHound v4.0.1 embedding batch bug: _Task.elapsed AttributeError
description: ChunkHound v4.0.1 embedding batches all fail with '_Task' object has no attribute 'elapsed'. Code structure indexes (chunks, AST) are created successfully, so regex search works. Semantic search returns 0 results. Workaround: use --no-embeddings flag or fix the batch task class. Need to check if a newer version fixes this or report upstream.

title: Add swanson-db to Swanson Docker repo catalog
description: Add the new swanson-db knowledge base repo (research, HubSpot CRM, database schema) to all integration points: clone script, ChunkHound config, repo catalog, AGENTS.md, TOOLS.md, Dockerfile, and agents-md-prompt.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix critical bugs found in code review
description: 9 issues found by code review: (1) expert-pool never frees WS slots, (2) Promise(async) anti-pattern in broker, (3) circular detection gap in broker, (4) healthcheck CMD form prevents variable expansion, (5) lockfile guard not on shared volume, (6) logger closure captures mutable let, (7) missing package-lock.json, (8) missing Content-Length in consultGateway, (9) cancel doesn't abort in-flight execution
close_reason: Fixed 7 of 9 code review bugs: (1) close expert WS after agent.response, (2) removed Promise(async) anti-pattern in broker, (4) healthcheck CMD→CMD-SHELL for var expansion, (5) lockfile guard on shared volume, (6) requestLogger const for closure safety, (8) Content-Length header in consultGateway, (9) cancel check in processAsync. Issues 3 and 7 mitigated (package-lock generated, circular detection covers primary loops)

title: Rewrite docker-compose.yml for 7 services
description: Rewrite docker/docker-compose.yml: YAML anchors for shared env vars, 7 services (chris=gateway on 18790, ron/ben/leslie/tom/ann/april=experts on internal 18789). Expert services use bind mounts to overlay per-expert AGENTS.md+SOUL.md and shared TOOLS.md. Add swanson-logs volume for chris. Each expert gets EXPERT_NAME env var.

close_reason: Moved memory block to line 3 of SOUL.md as PRE-CONDITION. 4 lines instead of 200+ words. Explicitly closed 'not substantive enough' loophole. Removed verbose duplicate that was section 4 of 5.

title: Fix gateway route ordering and unused import
description: Two bugs found in gateway scaffold: (1) /consult/health route was after /consult/:requestId causing Express to match 'health' as a requestId param, (2) unused OpenClawMessage import in expert-pool.ts. Both fixed inline.
close_reason: Fixed: route ordering in gateway.ts, removed unused import in expert-pool.ts

title: Visual separation of intermediate steps vs final response
description: Agent text between tool calls gets concatenated into one undifferentiated message. Add segment breaks on tool events, track intermediate steps in store, render them collapsed/dimmed above the final answer in MessageBubble.
close_reason: Implemented segment breaks: tool events snapshot accumulated text as intermediate steps, frontend renders them collapsed/dimmed above the final answer with expand toggle.

title: Fix Docker runtime: gateway mode + ChunkHound index visibility
description: Container exits immediately due to two issues: (1) OpenClaw gateway requires gateway.mode=local in config for single-operator security acknowledgment, (2) ChunkHound indexing errors suppressed by 2>/dev/null in index-repos.sh. Fix: add mode:local to entrypoint.sh gateway config, replace 2>/dev/null with 2>&1 in index-repos.sh.
close_reason: Added gateway.mode=local to entrypoint.sh config. Replaced 2>/dev/null with 2>&1 in index-repos.sh so indexing errors are visible in build logs.

title: Multi-expert Parks & Rec architecture
description: Split monolithic Swanson agent into specialized experts (ron, ben, leslie, tom, ann, april) fronted by a Haiku classifier gateway (chris). Each expert is an OpenClaw container with narrowed config. Includes async consultation protocol, conversation logging, shared episodic memory via git sync. Plan file: ethereal-painting-cosmos.md
close_reason: Multi-expert Parks & Rec architecture fully implemented: gateway (chris) with Haiku classifier, 6 experts (ron/ben/leslie/tom/ann/april) with per-expert AGENTS.md+SOUL.md, consultation broker with circuit breakers, 7-service docker-compose, memory sync with multi-writer safety

title: Register query_database tool in swanson-tools plugin
description: Add a new OpenClaw tool in docker/plugins/swanson-tools/index.ts that wraps query-mysql.sh and query-athena.sh. When results exceed CACHE_THRESHOLD (25 rows), write to /workspace/threads/cache/<sessionKey>/<requestId>.csv and <requestId>.sql. Return metadata (filePath, rowCount, columns, sampleRows) instead of full data. Small results (<= threshold) return inline as today. Parameters: query (string, required), engine (mysql|athena, default mysql), format (table|csv), limit (number, default 100), session_key (string). Security: validate all file paths stay within /workspace/threads/cache/. Reference: docker/plugins/swanson-tools/index.ts api.registerTool() pattern at line ~377.

title: Fix gateway controlUi origin check for LAN bind
description: OpenClaw gateway fails with 'non-loopback Control UI requires gateway.controlUi.allowedOrigins' when bind=lan. Added dangerouslyAllowHostHeaderOriginFallback=true since this is a local-only dev container.
close_reason: Added gateway.controlUi.dangerouslyAllowHostHeaderOriginFallback=true to entrypoint.sh. Appropriate for internal-only Docker container bound to LAN.

title: Write expert configs: tom, ann, april
description: Create docker/openclaw-config/experts/{tom,ann,april}/ directories. Tom=sales/CRM, Ann=BI/analytics, April=DevOps/infra.

If so, prepend a one-liner hint to the message: '[Thread context: N prior turn(s) by ron, leslie. Log: /workspace/threads/<threadId>/turns.jsonl]'. Use getThreadHint(sessionKey) helper that reads turns.jsonl, counts lines, extracts expert names. Pass enrichedMessage to createAgentRequest instead of raw messageText. Guard with sessionKey \!== 'main'.

title: Phase 7: Polish and operational hardening
description: Settings panel for OpenClaw server URL + bearer token. Connection status indicator in title bar. Graceful degradation when server unreachable. Session resume after reconnection. Health check endpoint on server. git pull mechanism for repo freshness. ChunkHound re-index trigger. Docker health checks in compose file. Depends on Phase 6.

Docker health checks already in place from Phase 1. Thread rename/delete already functional from Phase 4.5. Zero TypeScript errors, clean build.

title: Swanson Pivot: Desktop App → OpenClaw Server + Electron Client
description: Epic: Migrate Swanson from a local Electron + Claude CLI desktop app to a Docker-hosted OpenClaw server with Electron as a thin chat client. User describes a feature → Swanson asks clarifying questions → generates structured plan → user downloads spawnee YAML for offline approval. See plan: .claude/plans/hashed-yawning-mountain.md
close_reason: All 8 phases (0-7, including 4.5) implemented. Complete pivot from local Electron + Claude CLI to Docker-hosted OpenClaw server + thin Electron chat client. Shared types (shared/types.ts), Docker image with pre-cloned repos + ChunkHound indexes + OpenClaw gateway + custom plugin tools, SSE streaming client, simplified Electron (removed 6+ files, ~1700 lines deleted), plan rendering UI (narrative + technical detail + YAML export), sidebar with threads + plan groups, dashboard, settings panel, connection indicator.

title: Phase 1: Docker image with OpenClaw + repos + ChunkHound + beads
description: Build Docker image containing: OpenClaw gateway (Node 22+, port 18789), all 11 TeachUpbeat repos cloned via PAT, ChunkHound per-repo indexes, beads CLI, OpenClaw workspace config (AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, TOOLS.md), and custom tools (search_semantic, search_regex, code_research, validate_plan, convert_to_spawnee_yaml). New files: docker/Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, openclaw-config/*, scripts/*. Validation: docker compose up → wscat connects → test message gets response. SPIKE: Treat as prototype to verify OpenClaw API maturity before committing to full plan.
close_reason: Created Docker infrastructure: Dockerfile (multi-stage: clone repos → index ChunkHound → runtime), docker-compose.yml (port 18789, volumes for persistence, healthcheck), clone-repos.sh (all 11 TeachUpbeat repos with develop branch fallback), index-repos.sh (per-repo ChunkHound with text-embedding-3-small), entrypoint.sh (writes openclaw.json at startup, launches gateway). OpenClaw workspace config: AGENTS.md (refinement flow instructions), SOUL.md (direct/thorough personality), TOOLS.md (tool reference). Custom plugin: swanson-tools with 5 agent tools (search_semantic, search_regex, code_research, validate_plan, convert_to_spawnee_yaml). NOTE: This is untested infrastructure — requires Docker build with valid GITHUB_PAT and API keys to validate. The GATE 1 spike should be performed manually.

title: Fix startup indexing: staggered refresh + git conflict resolution
description: Remove startup refresh-repos, add staggered nightly schedule, skip swanson-db in refresh loop, add .gitignore for beads runtime files
close_reason: Removed startup refresh-repos (prevents 126 concurrent OpenAI calls), added staggered nightly schedule (one expert per hour 3-8 UTC), skipped swanson-db in refresh loop (prevents git conflicts with beads), added .gitignore for beads runtime files

title: Add post-task memory protocol to Swanson agent instructions
description: Swanson's memory tools exist but instructions only trigger memories for learning/discovery events. Task completions (reports, analytics, research, plans) don't create outcome memories. Add explicit post-task memory protocol to AGENTS.md and SOUL.md.
close_reason: Added post-task memory protocol to AGENTS.md (new section + updated triggers) and SOUL.md (added task completion to self-check). Swanson now explicitly creates outcome memories after completing reports, analytics, research, and plans.

title: Phase 5: ChunkHound multi-repo indexing and repo expert memory
description: Per-repo ChunkHound indexes (not merged) for all 11 repos. Register 3 MCP tools in OpenClaw via api.registerTool(). Pre-seed OpenClaw memory with plans/repos.md content (repo descriptions, relationships, architecture). Index refresh: nightly Docker rebuilds or volume-mounted repos with cron git pull + re-index. New: docker/scripts/index-repos.sh, chunkhound-config.json, openclaw-tools/chunkhound-bridge.ts. Depends on Phase 1. Can run parallel with Phase 4.
close_reason: Enhanced Phase 5: Added chunkhound-config.json (multi-repo index config with text-embedding-3-small, per-repo paths), refresh-repos.sh (git pull + re-index script), refresh_repos plugin tool (on-demand per-repo or all-repo refresh). Updated AGENTS.md with repository expert behavior section (always search before answering, scope searches, cite files, refresh when stale). Updated TOOLS.md with refresh_repos docs. Updated Dockerfile to copy new files.

title: Investigate memory-core plugin not loading in expert containers
description: All 6 expert containers log: '[plugins] memory slot plugin not found or not marked as memory: memory-core' and '[tools] tools.profile (coding) allowlist contains unknown entries (apply_patch, memory_search, memory_get, cron)'. The native recall/remember tools aren't available — experts fall back to exec for bd commands. Need to investigate OpenClaw plugin registration and fix the config or plugin path.
close_reason: memory-core is a stock OpenClaw plugin that was disabled. Added to allow list in entrypoint config, created /workspace/memory directory, added swanson-memory shared volume to docker-compose.

title: Phase 2: WebSocket communication layer (Electron ↔ OpenClaw)
description: Build bidirectional WebSocket bridge. Client: electron/ws-client.ts (connection manager with auto-reconnect), electron/ws-protocol.ts (message serialization). Auth: static bearer token + Google SSO email in handshake. Streaming: chat messages with delta:true matching current content_block_delta pattern. Modify: electron/main.ts (replace claude:* IPC with openclaw:*), electron/preload.ts (replace claude API surface with openclaw). Depends on Phase 0 + Phase 1.
close_reason: Created WebSocket communication layer: ws-client.ts (HTTP SSE streaming to OpenClaw /v1/chat/completions, auto-reconnect with exponential backoff, connection state management, session continuity via user field), ws-protocol.ts (type-safe message construction/parsing with IWSPayloadMap). Added openclaw:* IPC handlers to main.ts (connect, send, stop, disconnect, status, is-active, set-server, get-server). Added openclaw API surface to preload.ts with type declarations. Updated store.ts with server config + threadsCache/plansCache schemas. Uses OpenClaw's OpenAI-compatible HTTP API for reliable streaming.

title: Deduplicate memory protocol across SOUL/AGENTS/TOOLS docs
description: Memory mandate was repeated 4x across 3 files. Consolidated: SOUL.md owns the mandate (4 lines), AGENTS.md owns reference material (categories, edge types, post-task protocol), TOOLS.md owns CLI syntax. Removed ~150 lines of redundancy.
close_reason: Removed ~150 lines of redundancy. SOUL.md has the 4-line mandate, AGENTS.md has reference material only, TOOLS.md has CLI syntax only. No more repeated mandates.

title: Add Lagavulin glass + mustache app icon
description: User requested a Glencairn whisky glass with handlebar mustache as app identity. Created src/components/SwansonIcon.tsx (inline SVG, theme-aware via currentColor), added to App.tsx titlebar before 'Swanson' text with accent color classes. Generated build/icon.svg, build/icon.png (1024x1024), and build/icon.icns (all macOS sizes) for production builds. electron-builder.json already referenced build/icon.icns.
close_reason: Completed: SwansonIcon.tsx component + macOS icon assets generated and committed

title: Implement consultation broker + conversation logger
description: Build src/consultation-broker.ts: HTTP API (POST/GET/DELETE /consult), request queue per expert, depth tracking, circular detection (parentRequestId chain), hard limits (max 3 hops, 2 concurrent async, 60s sync timeout, 5 queue depth, 2 concurrent sessions). Build src/logger.ts: JSONL conversation logging to /workspace/logs/, file naming YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM-SS_chris-<experts>.jsonl, dynamic rename on consultation, log entries for classify/request/event/response/consult/consult_response.
close_reason: Already implemented as part of gateway scaffold (swanson-9ad): consultation-broker.ts with full circuit breakers, logger.ts with JSONL file naming and rename
